pinWillDeduct: Warning: Entering PIN will DEDUCT money from your account.
nowAtShop: Now you are at a shop paying for groceries.
isThisSafeOrScam: Is this message Safe or Scam?
lookAtInbox: Look at your inbox. Which messages are dangerous?
scenarioDesc: Someone messaging you claims to be your relative in trouble. Read the chat and decide what to do.
youLostMoneyDesc: Scammers create a fake sense of urgency. NEVER send money without verifying by calling the person on their OLD number.
scamAvoidedDesc: Excellent! The 'Rahul' messaging you was a scammer. You saved your money by verifying.
neverSharePin: Never share your UPI PIN or OTP with anyone.
safeSpaceTip: This is a safe space. Nothing here is real. Practice without fear.
practiceTip: Take your time. Each skill is a safe simulation — nothing is real.
safetyTip: These are simulations. No real data is involved. Learn from outcomes.
createPinSub: Choose a PIN you can remember. Avoid simple patterns.
confirmPinSub: Enter your PIN again to confirm.
pinSetSuccessMsg: You created a strong, secure UPI PIN.
learnWhenPin: Learn when to enter your PIN and when NOT to.
weakPinMsg: Weak PIN! Avoid simple patterns like 123456 or repeated digits.
pinNoMatchMsg: PINs do not match. Try again.
oopsMoneyDesc: You entered your PIN on a request. This sends money OUT. Never enter PIN to receive money.
paymentSuccessMsg: You only entered your PIN because YOU chose to send money.
whichRealApp: Which one is the official WhatsApp?
realAppExplanation: The real WhatsApp is by 'Meta Platforms, Inc.' with 5 billion+ downloads. Fake apps have different developer names and fewer downloads.
whichRealUrl: Which URL is the REAL government website?
wantInstallGpay: You want to install Google Pay. You see these in the store:
friendSendsLink: A friend sends you a link to install a 'Free Recharge' app. What do you do?
speakWelcome: Welcome to SafeLy. Choose Practice to learn safely, or Safety to test yourself.
speakPracticeHub: Choose a digital skill to practice. We will guide you step by step.
speakSafetyHub: Test yourself against real scam scenarios. Choose a challenge.
speakPracticeUpi: Learn to set a strong UPI PIN and practice safe payments.
speakDetect: Tap on each message to practice finding safe and scam messages.
speakSimulator: Read the chat carefully. Decide what to do.
smSuccessMsg: You learned how to safely send money using UPI.
smLearn1: Always verify recipient name before paying
smSpeak: Learn to send money safely using UPI.
qrPlacePhone: Place your phone over the QR code
qrGalleryTip: Tip: Use this when someone sends you a QR on WhatsApp
qrSuccessMsg: You learned how to scan QR and pay safely.
qrLearn1: Always verify merchant name after scanning
qrLearn2: Check the amount before entering PIN
qrLearn3: QR codes at shops are generally safe
qrSpeak: Learn to scan a QR code and make a payment safely.
pwdSuccessMsg: You know how to create a password that keeps you safe.
pwdLearn3: Never use your name or birthday
pwdSpeak: Create a strong password that no one can guess.
iaSuccessMsg: You can now spot fake apps in the store.
iaLearn2: Real apps have billions of downloads
iaLearn3: Fake apps often have extra words in the name
iaSpeak: Learn to pick the real app from the store and avoid fakes.
orderBlinkitDesc: Learn to order items from a delivery app
obSuccessMsg: You learned how to search, add items to cart, and place an order on a delivery app.
obLearn1: Use the search bar to find items quickly
obLearn2: Always check item details and price before adding
obLearn3: Select payment and delivery address before placing order
obSpeak: Learn to order groceries on a delivery app step by step.
symbolLiteracyDesc: Understand common icons used in apps
slExploreTitle: Tap each icon to learn what it does
slQuest1: Where do you search for something?
slQuest5: How do you go back to the home screen?
slSuccessMsg: You now understand the most common app icons.
slLearn1: Icons are the same across most apps
slLearn2: Tap icons to discover what they do
slLearn3: Look for the magnifying glass to search
slSpeak: Learn what common app icons mean by tapping them.
slIconCartDesc: View items you want to buy
slIconMenuDesc: Open the app's main menu or navigation
slIconHomeDesc: Go back to the main screen
aadhaarDesc: Learn to protect your Aadhaar card
aadhaarSpeak: Learn to protect your Aadhaar card and mask your ID.
aadhaarSuccessMsg: You know how to protect your Aadhaar and use it safely.
aadhaarLearn1: Your Aadhaar is linked to bank, SIM, and government services
aadhaarLearn2: Masking your Aadhaar hides your personal info
aadhaarLearn3: Always use official UIDAI website to get masked Aadhaar
aadhaarFact1: Your Aadhaar number is linked to your bank account, phone number, PAN card and government services.
aadhaarFact2: Sharing your full Aadhaar number can lead to identity theft, unauthorized SIM swaps and financial fraud.
aadhaarFact3: Over 500 million Aadhaar numbers have been exposed in data breaches according to cyber security researchers.
aadhaarMaskedInfo: Masked Aadhaar hides the first 8 digits and is officially issued by UIDAI. It is legally valid as identity proof for hotels, verification desks and other non-sensitive use cases.
aadhaarOfficialTitle: Get Your Masked Aadhaar — Official UIDAI Portal
aadhaarOfficialDesc: The Government of India provides a free official tool to download your masked Aadhaar card from the UIDAI website.
aadhaarLinkNote: You will need your Aadhaar number and a registered mobile number for OTP verification.
aadhaarSim1: Visit the official UIDAI portal and click 'Download Aadhaar'.
aadhaarSim2: Enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number and complete the CAPTCHA.
aadhaarSim3Option: Choose which Aadhaar type you want
aadhaarSim3: Select 'Masked Aadhaar' to hide your first 8 digits. This is the safe option.
aadhaarSim4: Enter the OTP sent to your phone
aadhaarSim4Desc: An OTP is sent to your registered mobile number. Enter it to verify your identity.
aadhaarSimSuccessDesc: Your masked Aadhaar has been successfully downloaded. Use this version whenever identity proof is needed.
otpC1a: Share the OTP to save my account
otpC1c: Ask them to call back later
otpE1: Banks NEVER call and ask for your OTP. Hang up and call your bank directly.
otpQ2: The OTP says TRANSFER. What does this mean?
otpC2b: Someone is trying to take my money
otpE2: The OTP says 'transfer'. Sharing it sends YOUR money to the scammer. Always read the OTP message carefully.
otpSpeak: Someone is asking for your OTP. What should you do?
flQ1: Is this link safe to click?
flC1a: Yes, it says SBI so it's safe
flE1: The real SBI website is 'sbi.co.in' — NOT '.xyz'. Scammers use similar names. Never click SMS links.
flE2: Real government websites end with '.gov.in'. URLs with '.free' or '.com' are usually fake.
flSpeak: Can you spot which URL is fake? Look carefully.
faQ1: Which is the REAL Google Pay?
faC1b: G-Pay Money Transfer by GPay Services
faE1: The real Google Pay is by 'Google LLC' with 500M+ downloads. Always check the developer name.
faC2b: Ignore — free recharge apps are scams
faE2: There is no 'free recharge'. These apps steal data. Never install from random links.
faSpeak: Can you spot the fake app? Look at the details.
seE1: Scammers create URGENCY. Electricity department never asks payment via WhatsApp. Verify by calling official number.
seQ2: Is this a real police call?
seC2a: Yes, I should pay to avoid arrest
seC2b: No, police never asks for money on phone
seE2: Police NEVER asks for money over the phone. This is a classic authority scam. Hang up and call 100.
seSpeak: Can you spot when someone is manipulating you?
tourEmergencyDesc: Verified helplines for scams and crimes, always available.

emergencyContactsDesc: Tap any number to call directly. These are official Indian government helplines.
